gallery.text.1: Wooden Pattern for toolrest body. Top view.
gallery.text.2: Side view of pattern - prints were included in the pattern for two sand cores.
gallery.text.3: glass mould with wooden spacers for sand/sodium silicate sand cores. The Sodium Silicate was
made from reclaimed silia gel dessicant that was going to landfill. The cylindrical core (bottom of frame)
was cast in a mould that was made from PVC pipe.
gallery.text.4: Bottom view of casting - the cylindrical sand core was coated in a layer of ZAMAK. The riser can
be seen still attached, at the bottom of the casting.
gallery.text.5: Side view of casting - the riser, gates and sprue can all be seen attached to the casting.
gallery.text.7: Toolrest base, with toolrest fitted. Assembly is near complete and resting on the ways of the lathe.
gallery.text.8: Completed toolrest in use; a bolt and wooden disk has been used as a temporary clamp.
gallery.text.9: New castings for a permanent clamp and 'T-bolt' type assembly.
gallery.text.10: Toolrest with assembled clamp. Chips in the paint are testament to its having been put to use.
gallery.text.11: Clamp assembly mounted in slot in toolrest base.
